_article: XXVII (Completion Is Proven by Life) + XXXI (desire-driven)
_description: Multi-dimensional capability scoring for NPCs, replacing the binary complete/incomplete assessment. Under Article XXVII, an NPC is not 'done' because its file exists; it is done when it demonstrates life across these dimensions. Under Article XXXI, an NPC is not 'alive' unless it initiates. This schema scores both. This is DATA, not a Java class (Article XXVI).

_design_notes.purpose: The old metric ('57/159 NPCs have initiation_lines') conflated scaffolding with completion. This schema distinguishes: an NPC can have initiation_lines (scaffolding) yet score 0 on memory or growth (not alive). The score drives prioritization — low-scoring NPCs are the next work.
_design_notes.scoring_scale: Each dimension is 0-10. 0 = absent. 3 = scaffolded (data exists, not wired). 5 = wired (Java reads it). 7 = demonstrable (a player can experience it). 10 = living (it initiates independently and the player would notice its absence). A 'complete' NPC under Article XXVII scores >=7 on all dimensions.
_design_notes.living_threshold: An NPC is LIVING when it scores >=7 on dimensions 1 (reasoning), 4 (relationships), 5 (memory), 6 (growth), and 9 (initiation). Below that, it is furnished. This is the Article XXVII bar applied per-NPC.
_design_notes.not_a_quest_score: Capability is not 'how many quests does this NPC give.' It is 'how richly does this NPC exist.' A mortal villager with no quests can score high if their relationships, memory, and initiation are rich.

dimensions.name: reasoning
dimensions.description: Does the NPC reason about its situation, or execute scripts? CognitionDrivenGoal usage, IntentDecomposer depth.

dimensions.name: goals
dimensions.description: Does the NPC have persistent, canonical goals that shape its behavior across cycles? Not just sect_tasks.

dimensions.name: relationships
dimensions.description: Does the NPC have bidirectional relationships with other NPCs (not just relationship_to_wanglin)? Can it affect and be affected by them?

dimensions.name: memory
dimensions.description: Does the NPC carry memories of past events? Can it retell them with distortion? Does its behavior shift based on what it has witnessed?

dimensions.name: history
dimensions.description: Does the NPC have a pre-simulation history (background, origin, losses) that informs its present state?

dimensions.name: growth
dimensions.description: Can the NPC change over time — cultivation breakthrough, moral shift, relationship evolution, grief, ambition?

dimensions.name: perception_tiers
dimensions.description: Does the NPC present differently to cultivators of different realms (Article VI)?

dimensions.name: affordances
dimensions.description: Does the NPC expose unique affordances (Article XXVIII) that make it mechanically distinct from other NPCs?

dimensions.name: initiation
dimensions.description: Does the NPC initiate interactions (Article XXXI)? Does it have a desire_state? Does it fire social intents toward the player AND other NPCs?

dimensions.name: canon_fidelity
dimensions.description: Does the NPC's behavior match canon where canon exists? Is INFERRED content flagged? Does it avoid contradicting Er Gen?

scoring_template.name: string
scoring_template.total: int 0-100 (sum, but total alone is misleading — check per-dimension)
scoring_template.is_living: bool — true only if reasoning>=7 AND relationships>=7 AND memory>=7 AND growth>=7 AND initiation>=7
weakest_dimensions: string — the 2-3 lowest scores, which are the next work
